notes: Both configs hit the same misses: no explicit Phase 2-agent wording, no explicit 'Phase 3 is more important (not less)' wording, and no fully explicit non-negotiable triad language tied to Phase 2 findings.
This suggests eval-1 may still be over-indexing on phrasing-level assertions rather than behavior-level discrimination.
Assertion-9 now discriminates: with-skill passed the explicit Validation Plan named steps; old-skill failed it.
The SKILL.md handoff update appears effective for this assertion.

recommendations: Keep eval-2 assertion-9 as a core discriminator; it is now functioning as intended.

Refine eval-1 to be less literal-phrasing dependent: require concrete workflow decisions that old_skill is less likely to produce (e.g., explicit exploration outputs, risk-to-phase mapping, or mandatory question prioritization tied to unknowns).
Optionally add one negative assertion to eval-1 (e.g., fails if exploration is treated as optional in unfamiliar repos) to increase separation.
